title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 28, 2026: Indices Reach New Records Amid Inflation Concerns

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Exhibit Mixed Movements, Major Indices Close at New Records
items.summary: The U.S. stock market experienced a day of mixed movements on Thursday, May 28, 2026, with the S&P 500, NASDAQ, and Dow Jones Industrial Average all pushing to new all-time highs by day's end, largely propelled by strong corporate earnings and resilience in the technology sector. Despite this upward trend, underlying concerns persist regarding elevated inflation and geopolitical tensions impacting oil prices. Investors continue to digest recent economic data and Federal Reserve commentary. This report is for informational purposes only and is not financial advice.

items.statContext: Up 0.6% to a new all-time high
Up 0.8% to a new all-time high
Up 0.36% to a new all-time high

items.headline: Snowflake Surges on Strong Earnings and Amazon Deal
items.summary: Cloud software company Snowflake ($SNOW) soared 38% after reporting blowout first-quarter earnings, boosting its full-year outlook, and announcing a $6 billion deal with Amazon, benefiting significantly from the ongoing AI boom.

items.headline: Dollar Tree Rises on Q1 Earnings Beat and Raised Guidance
items.summary: Dollar Tree ($DLTR) shares climbed 18.2% after the discount retailer exceeded Wall Street's first-quarter earnings expectations and raised its full-year guidance.

items.headline: Best Buy Jumps on Better-Than-Expected Quarterly Results
items.summary: Best Buy ($BBY) surged 18% today after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter results, including a 2.0% increase in comparable sales.

items.headline: Kohl's Rallies on Strongest Comparable Sales Performance in Four Years
items.summary: Kohl's ($KSS) rallied 21.3% after the retailer posted better-than-expected quarterly results and its strongest comparable sales performance in four years.

items.headline: Photronics Tumbles on Weak Q2 Results and Guidance
items.summary: Photronics ($PLAB) fell sharply by 33.69% after forecasting adjusted earnings per share for the third quarter that missed average analyst estimates, following weaker-than-expected second-quarter results.

items.headline: Synopsys Declines Despite Earnings Beat Amid Cost Concerns
items.summary: Synopsys ($SNPS) was down 9% today, even after beating second-quarter earnings forecasts, as investor concerns over lower GAAP earnings and restructuring costs weighed on the stock.

items.headline: Braze Inc. Shares Fall Post-Earnings and Outlook
items.summary: Braze Inc. ($BRZE) shares were down 11% after the cloud-based software company reported its first-quarter results and provided its outlook.

items.headline: Everpure Slides as Guidance Falls Short of Expectations
items.summary: Everpure ($P) shares slid over 14% after the computer storage company issued full-year revenue guidance that implied slower growth in the second half of the year, falling short of analyst expectations.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Shows Persistent Inflation and Revised GDP Growth
items.summary: The personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index for April showed a seasonally adjusted increase of 0.4% for the month, with the 12-month inflation rate at 3.8%, remaining above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. The second estimate for Q1 2026 real GDP indicated an annualized increase of 1.6%, revised down by 0.4 percentage points from the initial estimate, primarily due to downward revisions in investment and consumer spending.

items.headline: Fed Leadership Faces Inflationary Challenges Amid Reform Plans
items.summary: Newly sworn-in Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh aims for significant reforms, including re-evaluating inflation metrics and reducing the Fed's balance sheet, but immediately confronts two concurrent price shocks from tariffs and the U.S.-Iran conflict. Meanwhile, St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem cautioned against relying on higher productivity growth to resolve current inflation issues.

items.headline: Technology and Healthcare Lead, Energy Shows Mixed Signals
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly software companies driven by AI advancements, continued to buoy market benchmarks and demonstrated a broad-based recovery. Healthcare stocks also saw gains, acting as a defensive play ahead of inflation data. The energy sector experienced some declines in the prior day's trading, influenced by rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions, though it remains a top performer year-to-date.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Continue to Drive Oil Price Volatility
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions stemming from U.S.-Iran conflicts continue to impact global oil markets, with Brent crude climbing 2% to $96.13 a barrel today. Rising oil prices contribute to inflation concerns and create headwinds for companies with high fuel costs.

items.headline: Earnings Season Highlights Strong Retail and Tech Performances
items.summary: Today's earnings reports underscored strong performances in retail and tech, with companies like Dollar Tree, Best Buy, Kohl's, and Snowflake exceeding expectations. However, some firms like Synopsys and Salesforce faced headwinds despite profit beats, signaling investor caution regarding specific company outlooks or competitive landscapes, particularly from AI-focused rivals.
